basic_details.job_title: BFUHS Pharmacist Recruitment 2026 – 454 Pharmacy Officer Posts, Apply Online by 1 July 2026
basic_details.short_information: Baba Farid University of Health Sciences (BFUHS), Faridkot has released Advertisement No. BFU-26/20 inviting online applications for 454 posts of Pharmacist (Pharmacy Officer) under the Department of Health and Family Welfare, Punjab. The online application window is open from 11 June 2026 to 1 July 2026 at bfuhs.ac.in. This is a significant government pharmacy recruitment in Punjab comprising both fresh posts and backlog vacancies across multiple reservation categories.

application_fee.details: The application fee is non-refundable and must be paid online through the BFUHS portal. Candidates should select their category carefully at the time of applying, as the category cannot be changed after submission under any circumstances.

qualification.details: To be eligible for BFUHS Pharmacist Recruitment 2026, candidates must satisfy all three qualification conditions simultaneously. The eligibility is determined as on the last date of application, i.e., 1 July 2026. | Passed Senior Secondary Part-II (Class 12) with Science or its equivalent from a recognised university or institution; AND Possess a Diploma in Pharmacy or Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharm) or Pharm.D (Doctor of Pharmacy) from a recognised university or institution, or higher qualification in the same discipline; AND Registered as a Pharmacist with the Punjab Pharmacy Council set up under the Pharmacy Act, 1948 | Certificates and diplomas from non-recognised institutions will not be accepted and candidature will be declared ineligible. Foreign university degrees require equivalence/recognition proof from the University Grants Commission. | No person shall be appointed by direct recruitment unless they have passed the Matriculation examination with Punjabi as a compulsory or elective subject, or an equivalent Punjabi language examination. Additionally, as per Punjab Government Notification No. GSR72/Const./Art.309/Amd.(22)/2022 dated 28.10.2022, candidates must pass a qualifying Punjabi Language test equivalent to Matriculation standard with at least 50% marks, conducted by the recruiting agency alongside the competitive exam. Failure to secure 50% in this test will disqualify the candidate regardless of their other scores. | Exception: War Heroes discharged from defence/paramilitary services on account of disability (or their widows/dependents appointed under Government instructions) are exempt from the Punjabi language requirement. Wards of defence personnel who are bonafide Punjab residents must qualify the Punjabi test within two years of appointment. | Candidates wondering about age eligibility can note that the age limit for BFUHS Pharmacist 2026 is between 18 and 37 years as on 1 January 2026. Age relaxations are available for several categories as detailed below. | DON'T MISSRRB 6565 Technician Online Form 2026 (Short Notice)6,565 Posts • Technician • RRB29 Jul 20266,565 postsOSSSC 5989 Nursing Officer Online Form 20265,989 Vacancies • Nursing Officer13 Jul 20265,989 postsGSRTC 4599 Driver Online Form 20264,599 Openings • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,599 postsGSRTC 4318 Conductor Online Form 20264,318 Posts • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,318 postsKSP Civil Police Constable Online Form 20263,991 Vacancies • Civil Police Constable • KSP03 Jul 20263,991 postsMPSC 2619 Group C Online Form 20262,619 Openings • Group C17 Jul 20262,619 posts

salary_details.details: Selected candidates will be entitled to pay scales and emoluments as per Punjab Government instructions. The initial pay for the Pharmacist (Pharmacy Officer) post is fixed at ₹29,200/- (Pay Level 5 under the 7th Central Pay Commission). Other allowances and service conditions are governed by the Department of Health and Family Welfare Technical Group 'C' Service Rules of 2016 (with first amendment dated 23.07.2025) and relevant Finance Department instructions.

selection_process: Selection for BFUHS Pharmacist 2026 is based on a written examination (90 marks) combined with experience marks (10 marks)
giving a total of 100 marks. The written test is conducted by Baba Farid University of Health Sciences
Faridkot) Experience Marks – 10 Marks (as per Punjab Government

Letter Memo No. 1037633: HFW-HLH5021/161/2025-2H5/1/1329287/2026 dated 26.02.2026) Punjabi Language Qualifying Test – Mandatory qualifying test (minimum 50% marks required; failure disqualifies candidate) Document Verification – Certificate verification after written test Police Verification – Required before appointment; must produce Police Verification Certificate and clearance of criminal charges Final Merit List – Combined and category-wise merit list prepared on inter-se merit of written test and experience marks
Minimum qualifying marks in written test: 33%. Candidates scoring below 33% in the written examination will be declared ineligible for selection. In case of a tie in marks
preference will be given to the elder candidate. Higher qualification carries no extra weightage.
Candidates who served under the Punjab Health Department or its various institutions/agencies during the COVID-19 pandemic (March 2020 to June 2021
the period of lockdown/partial lockdown in Punjab) are eligible for experience marks. The criteria are as follows:
COVID service of less than one year: 5 marks COVID service of one year or more: 10 marks

Key conditions for experience marks: The written exam retains a maximum of 90 marks; experience benefit is capped at 10 marks. COVID experience must be verified by the Head of the concerned institution. The experience benefit applies only to Group-C and Group-D cadre vacancies. The benefit is available only when the candidate is applying for a vacancy of the same nature/job responsibilities for which they worked on contract/outsourcing under the Health Department during the pandemic. This benefit is applicable only for direct recruitment vacancies in the current year (up to 31.12.2026).

physical_eligibility.details: With 454 posts across Punjab's government health network, BFUHS Pharmacist Recruitment 2026 is one of the largest pharmacy recruitment drives in Punjab in recent years. General category has the highest allocation at 136 posts, while ESM/LDESM categories together account for 88 posts. The combination of written exam (90 marks) and experience marks (10 marks) means candidates with valid COVID-19 service experience under the Punjab Health Department gain a direct scoring advantage. The mandatory 50% Punjabi Language qualifying test is a key eliminator - candidates must prepare specifically for this component alongside the technical pharmacy exam. Minimum 33% in written exam is needed to even qualify for the merit list.

how_to_apply: Applications for BFUHS Pharmacist Recruitment 2026 must be submitted online only through the official university website. No offline applications will be accepted. Candidates should read the complete notification on bfuhs.ac.in before applying.
Navigate to the Recruitment / Vacancies section
Click on the link for Advt. No. BFU-26/20 – Pharmacist (Pharmacy Officer)
Register and fill in the online application form carefully
Select your category carefully - category once selected cannot be changed under any circumstances
Mention your sub-category vis-à-vis Caste in the online form
Upload all required documents including educational certificates, Punjab Pharmacy Council registration, and category certificates
Submit the application and download the confirmation receipt

Important: Candidates applying for more than one post must apply separately with separate fees for each post. No TA/DA will be paid for travel to the written test or counselling. The qualification including Punjab Pharmacy Council registration must be prior to the last date of filling the application (1 July 2026).

_labels.notification_pdf: Download Official Notification – BFUHS Advt. No. BFU-26/20

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for BFUHS Pharmacist Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for BFUHS Pharmacist (Pharmacy Officer) Recruitment 2026 is 1 July 2026. Applications must be submitted at bfuhs.ac.in.

faq.question: Q2. How many posts are available in BFUHS Pharmacist Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 454 posts are available - comprising 348 fresh vacancies and 106 backlog posts — across multiple reservation categories under the Punjab Health and Family Welfare Department.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for BFUHS Pharmacist 2026?
faq.answer: The age limit is 18 to 37 years as on 1 January 2026. SC/BC candidates get 5 years relaxation, Physically Handicapped get 10 years, and government employees can apply up to 45 years.

faq.question: Q4. What qualifications are required for BFUHS Pharmacist post?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed Class 12 with Science, hold a Diploma in Pharmacy or B.Pharm or Pharm.D from a recognised institution, and be registered with the Punjab Pharmacy Council under the Pharmacy Act, 1948.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for BFUHS Pharmacist Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is ₹2,360/- for all categories except SC (₹2,000 + 18% GST) and ₹1,180/- for SC category candidates (₹1,000 + 18% GST). The fee is non-refundable.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary for Pharmacist (Pharmacy Officer) in Punjab?
faq.answer: The initial pay is ₹29,200/- at Pay Level 5 under the 7th Central Pay Commission. Additional allowances are governed by Punjab Government and Department of Health service rules.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for BFUHS Pharmacist 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is based on a written exam (90 marks) + experience marks (10 marks) = 100 marks total, plus a mandatory Punjabi Language qualifying test (minimum 50% required). Final selection is on inter-se merit subject to Punjab Government reservation rules.

faq.question: Q8. Is there a Punjabi language requirement for this post?
faq.answer: Yes. Candidates must pass a Punjabi Language qualifying test at Matriculation level with at least 50% marks. Failing this test disqualifies the candidate from the final merit list regardless of their performance in other papers.

faq.question: Q9. Who conducts the written exam for BFUHS Pharmacist Recruitment?
faq.answer: The written examination is conducted by Baba Farid University of Health Sciences (BFUHS), Faridkot. Candidates scoring below 33% in the written test will be declared ineligible.

faq.question: Q10. What is the official website to apply for BFUHS Pharmacist 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply online at www.bfuhs.ac.in.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for BFUHS Pharmacist Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for BFUHS Pharmacist (Pharmacy Officer) Recruitment 2026 is 1 July 2026. Applications must be submitted at bfuhs.ac.in.
